signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7053 reflects a dominant G10 theme as USD outperforms (+0.676% avg) while AUD lags (-0.648% avg). Despite a 72bp OIS carry advantage for AUD, the pair is pressured by a sharp divergence in economic surprises: USD CESI net +29 (strong beats) vs AUD CESI net -8 (misses). Institutional positioning remains heavily long AUD (COT LF net +56,800, 76.5% long), creating significant liquidation risk if US CPI on June 10 surprises to the upside. The 1.324% G10 spread confirms a high-conviction bilateral trend favoring USD.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.19 has breached the critical 160.00 threshold, a level previously associated with BoJ verbal and physical intervention. While the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, retail sentiment is at an extreme 85% short, diverging from the crowded institutional short (COT LF net -105,136). Options markets are pricing in downside protection with a -1.14 25d RR put skew. Sustained trading above 160.00 increases the probability of immediate BoJ action, especially ahead of the June 16 policy meeting.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + Real Rates
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5816 shows a stark positioning conflict: retail traders are 88% long (extreme) while institutional speculators are net short -27,797 (15.1% long). The RBNZ's real rate of -0.83% is the most accommodative in the G10, trailing the Fed's restrictive +1.23% real rate. This 206bp real yield disadvantage, combined with institutional selling, suggests retail is catching a falling knife. Downside momentum is likely to persist toward 0.5750 unless RBNZ shifts its neutral bias at the July 8 meeting.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Carry Advantage
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7974 is supported by a massive 375bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%). The options market is pricing in significant tail risk with IV at 15.4% vs HV30 of 6.7%, resulting in a +8.7% Vol Risk Premium (VRP). This elevated premium suggests traders are paying up for protection against a sharp reversal, likely tied to Middle East geopolitical headlines (Iran-Israel tensions). However, carry remains the dominant driver while VIX remains in 'normal' territory at 18.9.
